observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries in the books to arrive at the correct balances of the accounts and to present fairly the related accounts in the financial statements and submit to the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher for the adjustments made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already made an adjusting entry.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant made an incorrect adjusting entry. This observation was reiterated in Observation No. 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere to Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, by returning the unused balances of fund transfers to the source agencies amounting to ₱94,509.65. This action ensures compliance with established regulations and promotes transparency and accountability in financial management practices.
observations.managementAction: The Management failed to return the unused balances of fund transfer to the source agencies by the end of the year.
observations.reason: The Management failed to comply with Audit Recommendation. This observation was reiterated in Observation No. 2.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the original Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation. Secure the addresses of the payees for notification of the return of stale checks in their possession for replacement.
observations.managementAction: The Management did not fully comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The Management failed to comply with Audit Recommendation. This observation was reiterated in Observation No. 5.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management grant cash advance to officers and employees for their travelling expenses only except for those designated special disbursing officers who may be granted special cash advances for other legally authorized purposes and strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 97-002 on the grant, utilization, and liquidations of cash advances and exhaust all possible means to settle cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The management through the Municipal Accountant issued demand letters to the employees concerned. The management is making every effort to comply with the rules and regulations governing the granting and liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Strictly take and exercise the necessary and appropriate actions to assert and enforce liquidation/collection of what has been due to the account of the LGU and institute further sanctions and remedies to successfully secure the return/refund of whatever sum of money or property misappropriated/misapplied as a result of improper, irregular, unlawful, and unauthorized use of LGU’s government funds.
observations.managementAction: The cash advance amounting to ₱300,000.00 was already liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of programs/projects/activities funded out of the 20% Development Fund to ensure that the expected benefits for the intended recipients are timely achieved or delivered. By focusing on the efficient execution of these initiatives, the municipality can fulfill its obligations to the community and facilitate the timely realization of socio-economic benefits. This approach will help address the shortcomings identified and enhance the effectiveness of the municipality's development efforts.
observations.managementAction:
The medical 
equipment valued  at P1,350,000.00 has already been purchased and is currently in use at Delfin Albano Memorial Hospital.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Management for ensuring that for CY 2023, all PPAs chargeable against the 20% Development Fund were in compliance with Item 3.2.5 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

Moreover, we acknowledged the Management’s effort for an exceptional accomplishment by utilizing the funds substantively in the attainment of the desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes pertaining to the development projects as incorporated in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) and Local Development Plans (LDP) of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The management immediately implemented the remaining PPA in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to identify the nature and cause/s of the unreconciled balance totaling to ₱23,891,585.47 and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to present fairly the balances of RPT and SET Receivables and Deferred Income accounts
observations.managementAction: The Management reconciled the accounts and ensured that the rules and regulations governing the measurement and recognition of RPT/SET receivables will always be observed.

observations.recommendation: We commended Management for the prompt remittance of the taxes withheld during the current year to the BIR. Notwithstanding, we recommended that Management continually ensure the withholding and timely remittance of taxes in conformity with Revenue Memorandum Circular Nos. 23-007 and 23-2012, and BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.
observations.managementAction: The management continually ensured the withholding and timely remittance of taxes and will remain in adherence to the COA guidelines and regulations specifically the Revenue Memorandum Circular Nos. 23-007 and 23-2012, and BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Management for complying with the GSIS regulations during the year on the deduction of personal share and loan amortization from the salaries of employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality pledge to sustain the commendable practices for the betterment of every employee while ensuring their compliance pursuant to Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291, otherwise known as “The Government Service Insurance System Act of 1997”.

observations.recommendation: We commended Management in complying with the provisions of R.A. No. 9679 on the withholding and prompt remittance of contributions and loan amortizations, and advised the continued observance of the requirements of the Act.
observations.managementAction: The Management will persist in maintaining their commendable practices for the well-being of every employee while conforming with Republic Act (R.A) No. 9679.

observations.recommendation: We commended Management for the faithful compliance with the withholding and prompt remittance of premiums to the PhilHealth and recommended the continual observance thereof for the benefits of its employees
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to consistently and accurately deduct and promptly remit PhilHealth premiums in accordance with Republic Act No. 10606.

Require concerned Department Heads to strictly/religiously follow the process as required under relevant rules and regulations in the measurement and recognition of RPT/SET Receivables; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to record prior years’ annual Real Property Tax receivable and Special Education Tax receivable.

observations.managementAction: The Management already set-up RPT/SET receivables and ensured that the rules and regulations governing the measurement and recognition of RPT/SET receivables will always be observed.

observations.recommendation:

Such policies shall include enforcement of waterline disconnection to avoid accumulation of uncollected water bills. Moreover, management of water system must maintain its independence as a separate cash-generating unit and improve its competence and integrity in recording and reporting all its transactions so that the public and other stakeholders may not deprived from adequate information on the result of the operations.

Update business policies/strategies/remedies to be applied to New and Old Market stall occupants. Also, apply legal remedies to enforce collection, if necessary; and

Instruct the Market Supervisor to be more efficient and submit documentations on the actions taken concerning collections from stall rentals.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up in the books the remaining amount of interest payable incurred in connection with the acquisition of long-term loans from the MDFO and the LBP so as not to understate the Payable account. Likewise, reclassify the entry taking up interest payment as Financial expenses but instead, as a debit to appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) account since the loans were used to construct/rehabilitate/expand capital/fixed assets to come up with correct asset account balances.
In case of any other factor that affects the amount of interest to be paid due to the bank’s other consideration, the recorded payable will be adjusted accordingly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LGU-Delfin Albano include in the budget proposal the allocation of 5% of its annual budget to support its  GAD Plan, which shall contain in details the programs and activities for the whole year as provided in the Joint Circular No.2004-1 of the Department of Budget and Management (DBM), National Development Authority (NEDA) and National Commission on the Role of Filipino Women (NCRFW)     

Furthermore, instruct the GAD Focal Point Person and other concerned Official to implement the GAD projects and mechanisms to achieve the objectives of the government regarding the institutionalization of Gender and Development in the communities. Also, the GAD Council should strictly conform to the guidelines for the preparation of Annual Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report. The same should be geared towards the development of programs, activities and projects that promote gender-responsive governance, protect and fulfill women’s human rights, and promote women’s economic empowerment.

observations.recommendation:
1.5	We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Treasurer  record the financial transactions of the Municipality daily pursuant to Sections 26, 27 and 28 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II. Likewise, direct him to update and balance all the transactions and reconcile the same with the cash on hand as provided in Section 63 (D) of LTOM.

1.6	Also, all the Revenue Collectors should maintain Teller’s/ Collector’s Cashbook to record all collections received and all remittances to the designated Liquidating Officer in accordance with Section 63 (C) of LTOM.

2.3	We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Treasurer reconcile cashbook balances with the ledger balances at least once a month or in every quarter to effect timely adjustments in observance of the provisions of Section 181 of the GAAM, Volume I and Section 63 of the LTOM on the required reconciliation of cashbook balances as these are important control tools for cash.
3.8	We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Treasurer submit reports and supporting documents to the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

4.7	We recommended that the Management stop immediately the practice of making outright disbursements out of collections, and create a Special Cash Fund which amount will be determined by LGU management and concerned Administrative Officer of the Hospital as emergency expenses will be drawn therefrom so that it will pass through the usual disbursements procedures.

5.4	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the monthly Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and his own monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms. The collecting officers are also required to prepare their Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms on a monthly basis. The CRAAF together with all the RAAFs should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 51 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

6.7	We recommended that Management:
Require concerned Department Heads to strictly/religiously follow the process as required under relevant rules and regulations in the measurement and recognition of RPT Receivables;
Cause the posting of a Notice of Delinquency in the Payment of Real Property Tax in publicly accessible and conspicuous places within the Municipality in accordance with Section 254 of RA No. 7160; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger pertaining to RPT Receivables

8.7	We recommended that Management through the Inventory Team  submit the result of the physical inventory that will be used by the designated Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant as basis for reconciling their records. In case the concerned employees failed to render the required reports and maintain property records, enforce Section 122 of the P.D. No. 1445 which states that “failure on the part of officials concerned to submit required documents and reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements”.
8.8	Moreover, we recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments of the PPE accounts after the physical inventory and reconciliation of the accounts in the books and after the provisions of the necessary depreciation to present reliable balances in the financial statements.

10.8	We recommended that Management (a) provide for a motor pool wherein unserviceable properties as well as assets still use for operations could be properly secured and  safeguarded against the elements of wear and tear; (b) create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible for the evaluation of the reasonable resale value of the unserviceable properties listed in the Inventory and Inspection Report of  Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and the immediate disposal of said items; (c) promptly cause the sale of the unserviceable motor vehicles thru public auction in accordance COA Circular No. 86-264.

11.5	We recommended that the Accountant issue Certificate of Availability of Funds (CAF) for all contracts of infrastructure projects as mandated under Section 168, GAAM Volume I and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
12.7	We recommended that the Management through the BAC Secretariat submit the lacking documents for technical review and evaluation process for generating timely and relevant audit results.

13.9	We recommended that Management comply with the following:
Submit the Monthly Report Of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption every end of the month
Ensure that all supporting documents especially the Driver’s Trip Tickets (DTT) are properly and completely accomplished and transmitted for audit; and
Establish and implement effective control measures in the use of government vehicle and fuel consumption as provided in COA Circular No. 77-61

14.6	We recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant maintain complete SL for each special account and prepare the corresponding financial statements to form part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance to serve as basis of the management to evaluate and recommend corrective measures for the improvement of the local economic enterprises.

15.8	We recommended to the Management that:
a) the basic services/priority expenditures enumerated above should be considered in the implementation of fund so as not to deprive the constituents of its benefit.
b) the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the Local School Board should prepare its SEF budget for proper allocation of its fund and taking into consideration the allowable expenses chargeable against SEF or PPAs set forth on the provisions of RA 7160 and DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

16.8	We recommended to the Management the following:
Require concerned personnel to strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations prescribing the guidelines in the formulation of Annual GAD Plan and Budget, development of agency GAD activities, implementation and monitoring of the GAD Plan and Budget and the preparation and submission of GAD Accomplishment reports.
Utilize the GAD fund only for acceptable programs, projects, and activities that would address the identified gender issues in the locality based on the Magna Carta for Women- RA 9710 and its IRR, PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
Strive for the full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by utilizing the whole amount set aside for said purpose so that the desired outcomes are attained and benefits are enjoyed to the fullest by the intended beneficiaries.

17.6	We recommended that the Management comply strictly with Items 3.1, 3.2 and 3.3 of DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017, on the  utilization of the 20% Development Fund.
17.7	Stop the practice of identifying and implementing programs and projects that are not consistent/contrary in achieving desirable socio-economic developments and environmental management.

18.5	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer transfer the remaining unexpended balance amounting to P 937,463.90 from the General Fund to Trust Fund in compliance with the aforementioned provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002.

19.8	We recommended the following:
The Management  provides separate budget for the MDRRM Office for personnel services, MOOE, and capital outlay sourced from the General Fund to effectively carry out DRRM projects, programs and activities of the Municipality.
Create and appoint other statutory DRRM staffs who will assist the MDRRM Officer in the administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning.
Special Purpose Appropriation-LDRRM Fund be solely utilize for DRRM-related projects and activities as provided in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013. Administrative expenses be charged from the separate MDRRM Office budget.

20.7	We recommended that in the succeeding fiscal years, the Municipality shall provide sufficient appropriation in the Annual Budget for Senior Citizens and Persons with Disabilities as provided under DILG Memorandum Circular 2014-59 and RA 9442 amending RA 7277.

22.6	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor facilitate the creation and organization of LGU’s IAS to assist the Management in directing and strengthening of the Internal Control System of LGU-Delfin Albano in their operations and to achieve effective and efficient administration and functions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of Due from/Due to Local Government Units appearing in the books of Provincial Government of Isabela (PGI) against LGU-Delfin Albano and make the necessary/appropriate adjustment.

We   recommended that management follow strictly the provisions of Section 13, Chapter 3, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs, that one depository account be maintained per fund. The Municipal Treasurer should be instructed to close other depository accounts maintained with LBP & PNB for sub accounts in the General Fund.  The Treasurer and Accounting Office should likewise maintain subsidiary ledgers for the various sub funds in the General fund and these suffice to provide information relative thereto.  In this way, bank reconciliation process could be facilitated, the use of numerous checks and surcharges for non-maintenance of minimum bank balances, unauthorized fund transfer and erroneous entries be avoided.
